Article Title: Cuss remarks against CJI: Two law students arrested over courtroom disruption
Article Text:
The accused have been identified as Prabal Pratap Singh (24), a third-year law student, and Chander Bhan (23), a second-year law student, both from Lucknow University.

NEW DELHI: Two law students from Lucknow University have been arrested in connection with last week's disruption inside the Supreme Court, Delhi Police told news agency ANI.One of the accused allegedly used abusive language against the Chief Justice of India (CJI), threw papers inside the courtroom and disrupted judicial proceedings during a hearing before a Supreme Court bench.Delhi Police said an FIR was registered at Tilak Marg police station based on a complaint by Supreme Court security staff over the incident that took place on July 10 inside Court No. 13 during the hearing of SLP No. 31367/2026, Prabal Pratap & Another vs State of Uttar Pradesh through Commissioner.The accused have been identified as Prabal Pratap Singh (24), a third-year law student, and Chander Bhan (23), a second-year law student, both from Lucknow University.According to the FIR, Prabal Pratap, who was appearing as a petitioner-in-person, "intentionally disrupted the court proceedings by using abusive and unparliamentary language, throwing papers inside the courtroom and creating disorder". It further alleged that when security staff attempted to restore order and restrain him, he used force against a staff member, obstructing the discharge of official duties.The disruption unfolded before a bench of Justices K V Viswanathan and Alok Aradhe. Appearing alongside co-petitioner Chander Bhan, Pratap said: "Mr Judicial Servant, I order you to order registration of FIR against ACP, Vikas Nagar, Lucknow.""You are ordering us?" Justice Viswanathan asked, after which Pratap continued waving a 185-page set of documents related to allegations against Duplex Technologies. He then threw the papers into the air, scattering them across the courtroom, before allegedly using a cuss word directed at the CJI, prompting security personnel to escort him out.Despite the incident amounting to alleged criminal contempt, the bench did not initiate contempt proceedings. Instead, it dismissed the special leave petition, observing: "As far as the merit of this case is concerned, we have perused the records, we find no good ground to interfere with the impugned judgement/order(s). The special leave petition is, accordingly, dismissed."
Article Summary: Accused identified as Prabal Pratap Singh (24) and Chander Bhan (23), a second- year law student, both from Lucknow University. Accused allegedly used abusive language against the Chief Justice of India (CJI), threw papers inside the courtroom and disrupted judicial proceedings.

Article Title: From Rahul Gandhi to Vijay to Nadda: CJP invites leaders across party lines to back NEET protesters
Article Text:
Rahul Gandhi, Vijay (C), Jagat Prakash Nadda (R) (File photos)

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you support the demands for accountability regarding the NEET UG leak? Yes, absolutely No, I don't think so I need more information Undecided 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party on Tuesday wrote to leaders across the political spectrum, inviting them to join its protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar to stand in solidarity with youth protesting against NEET UG leak.The protest, led by climate activist and educator Sonam Wangchuk , who is on an indefinite hunger strike, has gained national attention. In its appeal, the CJP urged leaders, including former BJP president Jagat Prakash Nadda and Lok Sabha leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi , to support students' demands for accountability and justice over the alleged examination irregularities.One of the major demand of the protesting group is the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over NEET fiasco.In a post on X on Tuesday, CJP chief spokesperson Saurav Das said he and the outfit's founder, Abhijeet Dipke, wrote to political leaders on July 9 and 10, inviting them to join the group's peaceful protest against the alleged NEET paper leak, which has now entered its 25th day.The letters were sent to BJP chief Nitin Nabin, Andhra Pradesh chief minister N Chandrababu Naidu, TDP MP Lavu Sri Krishna Devarayalu, JD(U) Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Kumar Jha and YSRCP MP P V Midhun Reddy.Among opposition leaders, the CJP reached out to Congress leader Rahul Gandhi, Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, DMK leaders Kanimozhi and Tiruchi Siva, Samajwadi Party supremo Akhilesh Yadav and Rajya Sabha member Ram Gopal Yadav, Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut, Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah, National Conference MP Aga Syed Ruhullah Mehdi, RJD leader Tejashwi Yadav and MP Misa Bharti.The outfit also sent letters to TVK president and Tamil Nadu chief minister Joseph Vijay, Azad Samaj Party chief Chandra Shekhar Azad and AIMIM president Asaduddin Owaisi."Even before these letters, and in the days since, several political leaders from the list and their party members have extended their support to our youth movement. Many have visited Jantar Mantar, while others have invited our delegation for discussions. We sincerely thank each one of them for standing with India's youth," Das said.He said the organisation "continue to await responses from several others"."We again sincerely appeal to the leaders to keep aside their politics and lend their voice to the voice of the young. This movement is about an entire generation demanding accountability for examination paper leaks, recruitment failures, and justice and compensation for the families of students who lost their lives at the hands of this corrupt, broken education system," he said."History will remember who stood with the youth when they called for justice. We only appeal that all of us stand on the right side of history," Das added.In a separate post on X, Dipke said the organisation had taken its demands to leaders across the political spectrum."The Cockroach Janta Party has reached out to all political parties regarding our demand of sacking the education minister and Rs. 1 cr compensation to the families of students who died by suicide," Dipke said.The protest, which began on June 20, has drawn support from Opposition leaders, academicians, activists and public figures.Among those who have visited the protest site are former Delhi chief minister Atishi along with AAP leaders, Samajwadi Party MP Pushpendra Saroj, TMC MPs Mahua Moitra and Sagarika Ghose, CPM MP Amra Ram, veteran CPM leader Subhashini Ali, former Kerala ministers K K Shailaja, Thomas Isaac, K N Balagopal and P Rajeev, Tripura Leader of Opposition and CPM Polit Bureau member Jitendra Chaudhury, and Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Arvind Sawant.The agitation has also received support from actor Prakash Raj, Punjabi singer Kaka (Ravinder Singh), delegations from the Samyukta Kisan Morcha (SKM), the Assam Jatiya Parishad Youth Wing, and several academics and members of civil society.In the latest development, Dipke said former Delhi CM AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal and Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray separately called him to enquire about Wangchuk's health. According to Dipke, both leaders expressed concern over Wangchuk's deteriorating condition and extended support to the proposed march to Parliament on July 20.
Article Summary: The Cockroach Janta Party wrote to leaders across the political spectrum, inviting them to join its protest at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. The protest, led by climate activist and educator Sonam Wangchuk, has gained national attention. In its appeal, the CJP urged leaders, including former BJP president Jagat Prakash Nadda and Lok Sabha leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi.

Article Title: ‘Fake Silver’ row: Jammu court summons IO, seeks records
Article Text:
Representative image

JAMMU: A Jammu court has directed the crime branch inquiry officer to appear in person with the complete record in a case linked to the alleged “fake silver” offerings Rs worth Rs 500cr at Vaishno Devi shrine after objections were raised over the agency’s handling of a complaint seeking registration of an FIR.The case first came to light on April 21, when Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board sent around 20 tonnes of accumulated offerings to a govt mint for melting and storage.Officials conducting the assay were surprised to find that the metal contained only about 5-6% of actual silver. The govt mint further raised alarm about the presence of cadmium, which closely resembles silver in colour but is highly toxic.On Monday, chief judicial magistrate (CJM), Jammu, Munish Kumar Manhas, ordered the officer to remain personally present along with the relevant record on the next date of hearing, fixed for July 29.The court complaint was filed by advocate Deepak Sharma, who alleged no action was taken on his complaint submitted to the crime branch on May 9.The complaint has sought a detailed investigation into whether devotees had been sold fake or adulterated silver articles by vendors and jewellers, or whether genuine silver offerings had been pilfered at any stage after their receipt, including during inventory, weighing, storage, transportation, assaying or melting.
Article Summary: Court orders crime branch inquiry officer to appear in person with the complete record in a case linked to the alleged ‘fake silver’ offerings at Vaishno Devi shrine. Case first came to light on April 21 when Shri Mata Vaish no Devi Shrine Board sent around 20 tonnes of accumulated offerings to a govt mint for melting and storage.

Article Title: 'Will join Uddhav's Ram Raksha Andolan,' says Fadnavis, but with a question
Article Text:
'Will join Uddhav's Ram Raksha Andolan,' says Fadnavis, but with a question

Poll Vote & Share your view Overall, do you believe that political movements related to religious sentiments are beneficial? Yes, they unite people No, they often create division It depends on the context I don't think they matter 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis on Tuesday questioned Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray 's proposed "Ram Raksha Andolan", asking whether Thackeray knew the "Ram Raksha" stotra and understood its meaning."He is organising a 'Ram Raksha Andolan,' but does he actually have any knowledge about 'Ram Raksha'? I will certainly join his movement and recite the 'Ram Raksha' [stotra] as well, but my question remains: what kind of unique movement is this, led by someone who does not even know 'Ram Raksha' or understand what it entails?" Fadnavis said, as cited by ANI.Earlier this month, Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray criticised BJP over the alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement case, accusing the party of using the issue for political purposes and announcing a statewide "Ram Raksha Andolan" from July 5.Thackeray had held a meeting with Shiv Sena (UBT) MLAs and MLCs at his residence, Matoshri, in Mumbai's Bandra area.He told those attending the meeting that the agitation should be expanded to the district level, including cities such as Nagpur, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar and Ratnagiri.The alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple came to light on June 7. An FIR was registered on June 25 based on a preliminary report submitted by a Special Investigation Team (SIT) constituted by the Uttar Pradesh government.Eight people associated with the temple's donation-counting process have been arrested so far, and the investigation is underway.The case has led to a political confrontation in Uttar Pradesh, with the ruling BJP and Opposition parties accusing each other over the handling of the temple's finances as the SIT probe continues.
Article Summary: Maharashtra chief minister Devendra Fadnavis questioned Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray's proposed "Ram Raksha Andolan" He asked whether Thackersay knew the "Ramraksha" stotra and understood its meaning. Earlier this month, the UBT chief criticised BJP over the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case.

Article Title: 'Expelled him from AAP long back': How Kejriwal reacted to Tahir Hussain's conviction
Article Text:
Kejriwal reacts to Tahir Hussain's conviction (ANI image on right)

BJP leaders question AAP

Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) national convenor Arvind Kejriwal on Tuesday distanced the party from former councillor Tahir Hussain after a Delhi court convicted him in the 2020 murder of Intelligence Bureau (IB) officer Ankit Sharma during the northeast Delhi riots.Responding to a post on X by BJP IT cell chief Amit Malviya highlighting Hussain's conviction, Kejriwal wrote, "We expelled him from AAP long long back. Didn't he join one of chanda chor party's sister organization?"Kejriwal's remarks came hours after Delhi law minister Kapil Mishra questioned the silence of the AAP leadership following Hussain's conviction.Addressing a press conference, Mishra asked, "Why are Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia and Sanjay Singh silent after the conviction? Why have they not come forward and said anything on the matter?"BJP leader Gaurav Bhatia, while addressing a press conference earlier today, also targeted Kejriwal. He said, "Even today, Arvind Kejriwal is protecting Tahir Hussain... AAP MLA Amanatullah Khan says that the Court order is unfortunate... Is this not vote bank politics?"He further adds, "An atmosphere was created by Rahul Gandhi and Sonia Gandhi that Muslims' citizenship would be taken away because of this. Today, I will ask them how many Muslims in India have lost their citizenship? ..."IB officer Ankit Sharma went missing after leaving his home during the communal violence in northeast Delhi. His body was later recovered from a drain near the Chand Bagh area.The riots, which broke out amid protests against the Citizenship (Amendment) Act (CAA), claimed 53 lives and left hundreds injured.
Article Summary: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) national convenor Arvind Kejriwal distanced the party from former councillor Tahir Hussain after a Delhi court convicted him in the 2020 murder of Intelligence Bureau (IB) officer Ankit Sharma. Delhi law minister Kapil Mishra questioned the silence of the AAP leadership following Hussain's conviction.

Article Title: Senior BJP leader, former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passes away at 88
Article Text: BENGALURU: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88 at a private hospital in Bengaluru on Tuesday.A veteran leader, Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades.Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death of senior BJP leader and former minister Ramachandra Gowda, describing him as a "brother" and a longtime companion.In a post on X, Yediyurappa recalled their journey together since the Jana Sangh era, their efforts to build the party in Karnataka, and praised Gowda's administrative acumen and dedication as a minister.BS Yediyurappa tweets, "I have lost yet another brother. The news of the passing of Shri Ramachandra Gowda, my soulmate of decades, senior leader, and former minister, has brought me immense sorrow. The days of struggle—standing shoulder to shoulder since the Jana Sangh era, toiling day and night, building the party from the grassroots level together in the state—swirl before my eyes. As a colleague in my cabinet, I have witnessed his administrative acumen and dedication up close. "
Article Summary: Senior BJP leader and former Karnataka minister Ramachandra Gowda passed away at the age of 88. Gowda was associated with the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) for several decades. Former Karnataka chief minister and BJP leader BS Yediyurappa expressed grief over the death.

Article Title: Dombivli hospital attack case: Shiv Sena corporator gets bail
Article Text:
Ramesh Mhatre

Kalyan

:





A court on Tuesday granted bail to Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, who was arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and nurses of a civic-run hospital in Dombivli last week.Mhatre was remanded in judicial custody on Monday after his police custody ended. His counsel then moved a bail application before the court.Urging the court not to grant any relief, police said several criminal cases had been filed against Mhatre in the past. Defence lawyer A Y Patki said in almost all the cases cited by police, he was either acquitted or the proceedings quashed, and that only one criminal case was pending against him.Sources said while in judicial custody, the 72-year-old corporator was admitted to Thane Civil Hospital owing to health issues. Mhatre is said to be suffering from kidney and prostate ailments and was kept under medical supervision instead of being lodged in jail.Meanwhile, Sena’s Kalyan MP Shrikant Shinde said the party had already issued a show-cause notice to Mhatre. “Action has been taken against those responsible. No attempt has been made to shield anyone. Police have acted in accordance with the law.”On July 6, Mhatre and his supporters allegedly assaulted doctors and other staffers of Shastri Nagar Hospital over a purported delay in treating a pregnant patient. The attack triggered a two-day suspension of routine outpatient services at all civic-run hospitals across Kalyan-Dombivli.
Article Summary: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre was arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and nurses of a civic-run hospital in Dombivli last week. He was remanded in judicial custody on Monday after his police custody ended. His counsel then moved a bail application before the court.

Article Title: Unrest within Sena & BJP along with NCP, says MPCC chief Sakpal
Article Text:
Maharashtra Pradesh Congress Committee (MPCC) chief Harshwardhan Sapkal

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





Maharashtra Pradesh Congress Committee (MPCC) chief Harshwardhan Sapkal on Tuesday said that unrest was brewing within Shiv Sena and BJP along with the NCP.Referring to NCP leader challenging deputy CM Sunetra Pawar’s election as party’s national president, Sapkal said, “The balance between Mahayuti is disturbed and the unrest within the three alliance partners will soon come to the fore. ”Sapkal, while addressing media persons at Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, alleged that the state govt failed to prepare for poor monsoon and its impact on parts of Maharashtra.“The CM and other ruling party leaders only made speeches about the mitigation measures for El Niño impact. However, there is no preparedness at the ground-level yet. No policies have been made for reserving water for drinking needs. The farmers are supplied with bogus seeds in many parts,” he said.Referring to the loan waiver scheme announced for the farmers by the state govt, Sapkal said the roll out of the scheme is deliberately delayed. “After providing multiple reasons for implementing the scheme, the government is now seeking income tax details of the farmers. Some ruling party leaders are trying hard so that the desired details become unavailable,” he said.Sapkal also said that the RSS should show courage and expel its “members” involved in the Ram temple donation theft at Ayodhya. “The RSS in the past was feeling proud that its members were at the helm of affairs at the Ram temple trust. Now, when robbery takes place on the donation, RSS cannot only express grief,” he said.Sapkal said the party would intensify its agitation on different public issues and strengthen the network in days to come.
Article Summary: MPCC chief Harshwardhan Sapkal said unrest was brewing within Shiv Sena and BJP along with the NCP. He alleged that the state govt failed to prepare for poor monsoon and its impact on parts of Maharashtra. He said the party would intensify its agitation on different public issues and strengthen the network in days to come.

Article Title: Guest house manager held for theft from VIP suite occupied by IAS officer’s relatives in Sambhajinagar
Article Text:
The guests found that a travel bag had been disturbed, and its zip was open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring, worth Rs 30,000, were missing

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





The Begumpura police have arrested the manager of a contractor-operated VIP suite at a govt guest house for allegedly stealing cash and a gold ring worth Rs 44,250 from a room occupied by relatives of an IAS officer posted in the district.The accused, Kapil Deshmukh (33), a native of Hingoli currently residing at Roza Bagh, was employed by the private contractor managing the VIP suite. He was arrested after a case was registered under BNS section 305(a).According to the complaint filed by Mohammed Zubaidullah Mohammed Ahmedullah (48), a private driver from Nehrunagar, his relatives from Hyderabad had checked into the guest house on July 11 to attend a family function in the city.The police said around 15 family members locked their room and left for the event at 9pm. When they returned around 1.30am on Sunday, they found a travel bag disturbed and its zip open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring worth Rs 30,000 were missing.Investigators found no signs of forced entry and the room lock was intact, leading them to suspect the involvement of someone with authorised access to the premises. Inspector Pravina Yadav, of the Begumpura police station, said the accused was subsequently traced and arrested.“The accused, who was working as the manager of the contractor operating the VIP suite, has been arrested. Efforts are on to recover the stolen cash and gold ring,” Yadav said.
Article Summary: Kapil Deshmukh, 33, was employed by the private contractor managing the VIP suite. The guests found that a travel bag had been disturbed, and its zip was open. Cash of Rs 14,250 and a 2.5g gold ring, worth Rs 30,000, were missing.

Article Title: Deputy CM Eknath Shinde orders action in banner collapse, seeks audit of all hoardings in Thane
Article Text:
Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde has ordered the swift removal of all unauthorized advertising hoardings





THANE: Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde has directed the Thane Municipal Corporation to remove all unauthorized hoardings across the city without delay.He also issued instructions to conduct an immediate structural audit of all hoardings and advertising boards installed in the city to avoid any accidents during the monsoon due to strong winds and heavy rain.Shinde took serious cognizance of the Monday banner collapse accident in Thane and immediately spoke to the Commissioner over the phone directing him to take corrective action.Following these directives, the Thane Municipal Corporation launched an enforcement drive.Shinde warned that stringent legal action be taken against those who install hoardings or banners illegally and even those legal installations that are found to be unsafe during a safety audit.Meanwhile, the Naupada police late Monday booked an unknown person for the incident following a complaint by the Thane Municipal Corporation. Further investigations are on, officials said.
Article Summary: Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde has ordered the swift removal of all unauthorized advertising hoardings in Thane. He also issued instructions to conduct an immediate structural audit of all hoardings and advertising boards installed in the city to avoid any accidents during the monsoon.

Article Title: Doctor assault case: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, 3 others sent to judicial custody till July 24
Article Text:
Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) on Monday remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24





KALYAN: The Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) on Monday remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24 in connection with the assault on doctors and hospital staff at the KDMC-run Shastri Nagar Hospital in Dombivli.The hearing was conducted through video conferencing after the police cited security concerns, informing the court that a large number of Mhatre's supporters had gathered outside the court premises. Accepting the police's request, the magistrate permitted the accused to be produced virtually instead of being brought to court.Following the remand, counsel representing the accused filed a bail application before the court. The application is expected to be heard in the coming days.The case pertains to the alleged assault on doctors and hospital employees after a dispute over the delay in treatment of a pregnant woman at the KDMC run hospital.The incident had triggered widespread outrage, prompting doctors across KDMC-run hospitals and healthcare centres to suspend routine outpatient services in protest while continuing emergency medical care.Based on a complaint lodged by Dr Vaibhav Salunkhe, the Vishnu Nagar police registered an FIR against Mhatre and others under various provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and the Maharashtra Medicare Service Persons and Medicare Service Institutions (Prevention of Violence and Damage or Loss to Property) Act, 2010.Mhatre, a senior corporator of the Shiv Sena (Shinde faction), was arrested after the incident. During police custody, he was admitted to Thane Civil Hospital after complaining of chest pain and other health issues before being discharged and produced before the court through video conferencing which had sent him into four days police custody which ended on Monday.
Article Summary: The Kalyan Judicial Magistrate First Class (JMFC) remanded four accused, including Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre, to judicial custody till July 24. The case pertains to the alleged assault on doctors and hospital employees after a dispute over the delay in treatment of a pregnant woman at the KDMC run hospital.

Article Title: FIR against 4 content creators over posts targeting Nitin Gadkari on ethanol row; who are they
Article Text:
According to the complaint, Manish Kashyap uploaded a YouTube video on July 3, 2026, allegedly containing false, defamatory and misleading statements regarding Gadkari and E20 fuel with the intent to mislead the public.





NAGPUR: The Nagpur Cyber Police have registered an FIR against four social media content creators over posts and videos targeting Union road transport and highways minister Nitin Gadkari in connection with the ethanol-blended fuel (E20) debate.The case was registered on the basis of a complaint filed by BJP Nagpur City Social Media Cell convener Shishir Arun Tripathi, who alleged that the content contained false, misleading, defamatory and objectionable claims about Gadkari and the government's ethanol policy.According to the FIR, the four content creators booked are: Manish Kashyap, 'Desi Boys', Harshit Rathi and Anklesh Inwati.According to the complaint, Manish Kashyap uploaded a YouTube video on July 3, 2026, allegedly containing false, defamatory and misleading statements regarding Gadkari and E20 fuel with the intent to mislead the public.The complaint further alleged that similar videos and posts were circulated through the Instagram accounts 'Desi Boys', Harshit Rathi and Anklesh Inwati, making what the complainant described as baseless and objectionable allegations against the Union minister.According to the complaint, the content had the potential to create confusion among citizens and disturb public peace.The complaint also alleged that some of the Instagram posts used obscene language against Gadkari and included edited videos and images intended to disturb public order.Based on the complaint and digital evidence submitted, the Nagpur Cyber Police registered FIR No. 0092/2026 under Sections 356, 352 and 296 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023, along with Section 67 of the Information Technology Act, 2000.Cyber police inspector Sudhir Borkute has registered the case and initiated an investigation.The FIR comes amid a political debate over E20 petrol. Aam Aadmi Party national convenor Arvind Kejriwal has urged the Centre to reconsider the rollout of E20 petrol for vehicles not designed to run on ethanol-blended fuel.He has also demanded that petrol pumps offer consumers a choice between pure petrol and E20-blended fuel and called for a reduction in the price of E20 fuel, arguing that it delivers lower mileage.
Article Summary: Nagpur Cyber Police have registered an FIR against four social media content creators over posts and videos targeting Union road transport and highways minister Nitin Gadkari. The four content creators booked are: Manish Kashyap, 'Desi Boys', Harshit Rathi and Anklesh Inwati. The case was registered on the basis of a complaint filed by BJP Nagpur City Social Media Cell convener.

Article Title: TN turns to donors to fix govt hospitals
Article Text: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay presents an appointment order to a candidate during a recruitment event organised by the Department of Health and Family Welfare at the Presidency College in Chennai on Tuesday.
Article Summary: Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay presents an appointment order to a candidate during a recruitment event. The event was organised by the Department of Health and Family Welfare at the Presidency College in Chennai. The recruitment event was held to recruit doctors, nurses and midwives.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: Kanyakumari custodial death case: 3 wardens arrested
Article Text:
TOI

TOI





MADURAI: The Kanyakumari district police on Tuesday arrested two jail wardens and the chief jail warden of Nagercoil sub-jail for attacking a 35-year-old remand prisoner, leading to his death on Monday.The arrested were jail wardens Sivakumar , 48, of Swamiyarmadam, and Jegan , 46, of Thengaipattinam, and chief warden Thirumalai Nambi Suresh, 50, of Kalakkad.A senior police officer toldthat the deceased, Sabari Varman, was an alcoholic who behaved ‘abnormally’. Though he wasn’t drunk that day, he reportedly provoked other inmates. In order to bring him under control, one of the arrested jail staff allegedly beat him with his hands. The others were arrested for concealing evidence.“We have also identified five inmates who assisted in the crime. Necessary action will be taken against them after further investigation,” the officer added. Though autopsy on the body was done on Tuesday, his family is yet to receive the body. The officer said preliminary observation during autopsy revealed that injuries were present in his body.Sabari Varman, a disabled person from Ethankadu, was lodged in the Nagercoil sub-jail on July 9 in a case connected with the possession of 200 grams of gutka.He died on Monday after developing health issues, according to prison authorities. The family alleged custodial torture and held a road roko in front of Kanyakumari medical college hospital. A case was registered by the Nesamani Nagar police and an investigation is underway.A senior police officer told, “When he was remanded, the medical fitness by doctors didn’t mention any external injuries; the same was observed in the judicial magistrate’s statement.” The officer said the victim’s sister had mentioned in her complaint that he was doing well when she had visited him in prison.Meanwhile, DMK accused the TVK govt of engaging in diversionary politics. Party legal wing joint secretary I Paranthamen said, “While this incident was unfolding in Kanyakumari, the chief minister was busy filming scenes — such as touring the streets of Perambur and distributing rice at a ration shop — to keep the cameras focused on himself and divert attention from the actual event.”
Article Summary: The arrested were jail wardens Sivakumar and Jegan and chief warden Thirumalai Nambi Suresh. The deceased, Sabari Varman, was an alcoholic who behaved ‘abnormally’ Though he wasn’t drunk that day, he reportedly provoked other inmates.

Article Text:
Former AIADMK ministers Sellur K Raju and R B Udayakumar launched sharp attacks on Tamil Nadu chief minister Joseph Vijay





Madurai: Former AIADMK ministers Sellur K Raju and R B Udayakumar launched sharp attacks on Tamil Nadu chief minister Joseph Vijay at a party trade union meeting in Madurai, questioning his legitimacy and predicting that his TVK govt could collapse within months.“A chief minister’s position carries certain standards. Speaking as an actor is one thing, but an address inside the assembly should be delivered with civility,” said Raju. He also revived allegations concerning Vijay’s income disclosures for the film Puli and import of a luxury car. “What qualification do you have to call somebody else a thief?” Raju said.Udayakumar said the TVK govt could fall within three months because of the Karur CBI case, while also citing the possibility of simultaneous elections in 2029. “Prime Minister Modi is there. One Nation, One Election could be implemented in 2029,” he said, adding that the TVK govt could face an election before completing its term.
Article Summary: Former AIADMK ministers Sellur K Raju and R B Udayakumar launched sharp attacks on Tamil Nadu chief minister Joseph Vijay at a party trade union meeting in Madurai. Raju questioned Vijay’s legitimacy and predicted that his TVK govt could collapse within months.

Article Title: Inspector suspended after probe finds woman’s complaint genuine
Article Text:
The suspended officer has been identified as Ganganathapandian

Virudhunagar

:





A police inspector attached to the Aruppukottai town police station in Virudhunagar district has been placed under suspension following the completion of a departmental inquiry into a complaint lodged by a woman.The suspended officer has been identified as Ganganathapandian.Senior police officials told TOI that in 2024, he was posted as an inspector at Nazareth in Tuticorin district. During his tenure there, a woman had submitted a complaint alleging immoral activity by the inspector.Based on the complaint, the IG south zone Vijayendra S Bidari directed the Tuticorin district superintendent of police Abishek Gupta to conduct a detailed inquiry into the allegations. Following a preliminary probe, a formal departmental investigation was ordered.The inquiry found genuine in the allegations raised by the complainant.Acting on the complaint, DIG (Madurai range) Abhinav Kumar ordered the suspension of the inspector pending further departmental proceedings.
Article Summary: A police inspector attached to the Aruppukottai town police station in Virudhunagar district has been placed under suspension following a departmental inquiry. The suspended officer has been identified as Ganganathapandian. He was posted as an inspector at Nazareth in Tuticorin district.

Article Title: BJP councillor R Sugathan retakes oath in prison after court order
Article Text:
Thiruvananthapuram corporation councillor R Sugathan, currently detained under Kaapa, takes oath of office at Viyyur Central Jail on Tuesday





Thrissur: BJP local functionary R Sugathan, who is under preventive detention, retook his oath as Thiruvananthapuram corporation councillor at Viyyur Central Jail on Tuesday morning after a Kerala high court order permitted him to do so, arguably in a first such incident in the state. Mayor V V Rajesh administered the oath of office in the jail library. Sugathan took oath in the name of God.A history-sheeter facing 19 criminal cases, Sugathan was elected from Vazhottukonam ward.Sugathan has been lodged in the prison under Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act (Kaapa) in preventive custody for six months after its advisory board revoked his plea.Nineteen BJP councillors retook oath three weeks ago after HC nullified their first swearing-in procedure for invoking Bharat Mata, deities and other figures, in violation of Kerala Panchayat Raj Act, which allows only the name of God or solemn affirmation.
Article Summary: Thiruvananthapuram corporation councillor R Sugathan, currently detained under Kaapa, takes oath of office at Viyyur Central Jail on Tuesday morning. A Kerala high court order permitted him to do so, arguably in a first such incident in the state. Sugathan was elected from Vazhottukonam ward.
